title: Discover Sylamore Creek Camp: Our Honest Review of This Ozark Hidden Gem! 🌲🏕️🚵‍♂️
body: We travel up there all the time to visit the festival and love it there. Thanks for sharing this video, we were aware of the place but never checked it out. Our next trip to Mountain View might have us staying here. We have been going to the festival for years and have heard Sylamore pronounced both ways, guess it all depends on where you are from, some folks just get hung up on stuff like this....it drives me crazy...geez😊😊 Great video by the way.
author: @MikeWells-v4g

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body:
Were you surprised? Honest thoughts and reactions? What videos would you be curious to see in Harrison?
WATCH PART 2: https://youtu.be/4G2FtrWJrs8
author: @CantoMando

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: That old lady on the mobility scooter was so happy to see her 26 year old granddaughter's anime characters running around in real life.
author: @Staplegun

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: The grandma was normal for her age, old people can say weird things like kids, she was actually very sweet.
author: @ashishrain1455

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: I love how young people are conscious of the town's bad reputation, and try to change that by being nice to new people
author: @hekohh

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: I’m Asian…and I wasn’t offended by the lady in the chair at all. The lady in the chair didn’t seem to have any ill-will. She unfortunately just was never taught the best way to use the correct words and terminology with people of Asian descent/other ethnicities, or was never taught how to properly interact with people of other ethnicities in general. Her choice of words were really poor…but she had her heart in a good place and it definitely wasn’t “heckling”.
author: @fd3

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: Ngl the old woman shouting out her granddaughter was lowkey wholesome. I mean like she’s a little confused but she got the spirit.
author: @squidwardwithblondehair7219

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: Going to a Walmart in the most racist town in America is a terrible idea, but it helps to expose discrimination. Huge praise to you guys for being so brave!
author: @MahiAviation

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: The younger generation is usually more welcoming, but one thing to point out is that people behave differently to their true self when they know they’re being filmed
author: @lychee0733

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: When you encounter an NPC with only one line of dialogue: my granddaughter is twenty six and loves China men!
author: @DahmerBeMunchin

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: The funny thing about Chinese people, I’m Chinese, is that when we visit a place outside of home, we still try to find a Chinese restaurant.
author: @stephwyu

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body: You guys are so brave...I was born and raised in Chicago, and although most areas are diverse, I always got weird stares and occasional racist comments from strangers and classmates. I transferred to WIU in Macomb around covid time and omfg, I should've known but people would take one look at me and run away lol I'm glad you guys were welcomed and had a good time in Arkansas!
author: @enkarion01

title: Day in the Life of an Asian in America's Most RACIST Town
body:
Tourist: Every one is starring at us.

Harrison: This Town is as southern as it gets we're just happy to finally get some diversity.
author: @officialsquadrilogystudio

title: THIS IS MAJOR👑🥁#drummajor #shecanandshedid #homecoming  #football #queen (via arkansasstate/IG)
body:
My Sophomore year, our homecoming King was also on the football team AND in the marching band, he went from playing in the game, to doing the whole marching field show, and then had to hand his clarinet off to someone so he could literally sprint across the field to join the homecoming court for theur walk out. Plus, he was a super sweet guy, and he did it all with the same amount of grace and enthusiasm as this amazing young lady.

Some people truly astound me, especially at such young ages. That drive will take them very far in life ❤

title: STATE RECORD BASS LIVES HERE 😦 #bassfishing #shorts #fishing
body: Not criticizing, I think fish out of private ponds and lakes should not count for angling records. It's pretty much like the way high fence deer don't count. Should be more of an agricultural record. Nice bluegill though, pound for pound hardest fighters.
author: @jeremystanton382

title: STATE RECORD BASS LIVES HERE 😦 #bassfishing #shorts #fishing
body: I’ve been fishing a guys pond since I was 11 and I’m 24 now. He drained it like 4 years ago and put all new fish in there and the biggest bass in there now is close to 7 pounds they really do grow fast in ponds.
author: @CoreyCaudle-mp7zn

title: STATE RECORD BASS LIVES HERE 😦 #bassfishing #shorts #fishing
body: Good luck! Hopefully it makes it over 16lb 8oz!!! That’s a ways to go! And yes it would count, just looked it up, no pay lakes or fish hatcheries. Of course a private pond would count many records are from them.
author: @inthewoods3237

title: STATE RECORD BASS LIVES HERE 😦 #bassfishing #shorts #fishing
body: Had a 11-12lb largemouth in my property pond growing up. Algae bloom killed off pretty much everything 5lb and up
author: @TommyKamay

title: STATE RECORD BASS LIVES HERE 😦 #bassfishing #shorts #fishing
body:
My grandfather, Clint Durrance, had a bass
he caught out of an old phosphate pit and released in a pond behind his house. The fish got to be close to 10 lbs and would eat grasshoppers from his hand. Channel 13 and Salty Sol documented all of this.
author: @richardshirley2786

title: STATE RECORD BASS LIVES HERE 😦 #bassfishing #shorts #fishing
body: So in theory, the maximum size for a fish to grow would start out when it’s tiny. The hormones released from them as they grow stunt the growth of others. It’s a battle of dominance, and ponds have a hard time getting fresh water in and old water out. An excess of hormones are in the water, and it’s why was don’t get over 3 pounds in them. Any fish over that was likely put there from a larger body of water. They also travel through creeks and small streams, and even sewers when it rains. Try cooking all the small bass you catch in your trophy ponds. Anything under 16 inches
author: @GenghisKanghis

title: STATE RECORD BASS LIVES HERE 😦 #bassfishing #shorts #fishing
body: It’s harder than you think. I grew up on a dairy farm with 90 acres of lakes and still never caught a bass over 10 pounds and I finished most of my life on and off and almost every day when I lived on the dairy one of our buddies was fishing there one day and he did pull a bass out of there. That was a little over 10 pounds but it’s harder than you think to find one.
author: @Boarskiesrus

title: STATE RECORD BASS LIVES HERE 😦 #bassfishing #shorts #fishing

title: 72 Hours Inside State Trooper Academy | Ep.01
body: I have a friend in the police. He always stated he hated me, and when I asked why? He stated everyone of the guys from the military always hit the drills, and PT way easier than anyone in the class and academy. Which made both of us laugh. As a vet I thank you for this channel and your approach to the subject at hand.

title: 72 Hours Inside State Trooper Academy | Ep.01
body: There is a reason why Troopers act the way they do, the training is pretty legit and they keep it OG unlike basic LE academy
author: @buck6089

title: 72 Hours Inside State Trooper Academy | Ep.01
body: I’ve always wanted to be a Pennsylvania State Trooper. Many don’t know but the PSP was the first in the country. The PSP is rooted in history and tradition. Highly regarded by former President Teddy Rosevelt.
author: @BigHat_Actual1776

title: 72 Hours Inside State Trooper Academy | Ep.01
body: Austen, you have no idea how important of a video this is to me. I ship off in 2 days, and the fact that you're willing to put yourself through this to show the people how it really is goes miles out. Thank you for your content, it's really helped me make my decision to join The Army. Also, hella entertaining. It's been wonderful watching this channel since Unus Annus. Momento Mori
author: @thishandleisntavai1able023

title: 72 Hours Inside State Trooper Academy | Ep.01
body: My dad is a retired Pennsylvania State Trooper, so this video is so special! It’s so interesting seeing what he had to go through when he went to the academy in the 90s.

title: 72 Hours Inside State Trooper Academy | Ep.01
body: "she just walk behind like she is not scared, you never scare getting kick" HAHA doing horse riding for so many years I just freaking love Austen' reaction afraid about being kick by the horse I felt immediatly the moment he walked behind the horse at first he wasn't so confident to walk behind. Don't worry even here in France many people are afraid to walk behind horses

body: you always get yourself into amazing stuff and i love how you try new things and how well you handle any situation that's thrown at you.  you always rise to the challenge and the outcome never fails to amaze me there is nothing you cant do if you put you mind to it and you're proving that to the world. you're such an amazing man this world is blessed to have you in it .

author: @PeterSantenello

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: I love how Peter can go from a $3 million mansion to a $3 hot dog stand and treat everyone with the exact same level of respect.
author: @GamingEpochs

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: Man you have no idea what a blessing your channel is. I'm here at home after a long week of work in the office watching people on the other side of the planet, sipping on cold beer after I went to the gym. I am from Bulgaria, Eastern Europe and I just wanna say that I love America, I love the small parts and cozy towns. I love your channel because you show the REAL Americans and the REAL places. I am so sick of  the negativity of the media and all the negative things. American people are good people, America is beautiful and full of culture and nobody can convince me it is not.
author: @robert.ivanovs

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: Greetings from the Czech Republic! I can't travel, my wife and I are taking care of our severely disabled daughter Barbora. Thank you, Peter, for allowing me to travel in this way. I love the old architecture of America.
author: @janbenda5322

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: Seeing the Iranian community, the Hispanic community, and the locals in the 'hood' all showing such hospitality proves that we have way more in common than the media wants us to believe. Great job, Peter.
author: @GamingEpochs

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: Peter, you are an Ambassador of Kindness in this country, bringing us all together, connecting our communities and cultures and lifting the veil of false fears and propaganda that is always working to divide us. Sunday is a treat when we have a video from Peter Santenello. Much love!
author: @zariballard

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: I stayed the night in Little Rock driving home from San Antonio alone and every person I came into contact with was SO KIND. Delta region kindness and true southern hospitality is unmatched.
author: @savdeb7973

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: As a born and bred Arkansan,  I encourage you to get out in nature. That is the thing most people don't understand about our culture. We are still very outdoor ish. We flock to the rivers and lakes and forests. We camp, hike, bike, canoe, fish, and hunt and all this is practically free. You HAVE to see the Buffalo and the Ouachita rivers and Degray Lake and Petite Jean Mountain and the Crater of Diamonds  and Hot Springs and on and on We love our outside.
author: @carlahardwick9908

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: Imagine forgiving debt when you've done so well and can afford to? What a saint and a legend, shout out to the Dr.
author: @bend1499

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: I drove through Little Rock on the way cross country when I moved to California in 93. I was shocked on how much I liked Little Rock. Because the people were so kind.
author: @SamLed1111

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: The lady 24 mins in said the realest shit I've heard in years. Rare to see someone so self aware and profoundly inspiring
author: @amarmedic2521

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: Respect to Little Rock. We ended up there for a conference once (it was never on our radar) but now we’ve been back several times. The people are friendly and amazing, the vibes are good, and we have had a great time while feeling completely safe.
author: @Theartoftending

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: Peter you are by far the best in depth destination journalist ever.. Makes for a great Saturday.. Thank you & stay blessed 💙
author: @svgal1382

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body:
Watching from Sweden, and I just want to say how much I appreciate this channel. Peter has a rare gift for showing the world through real people, not headlines or stereotypes.

Every episode reminds me how big, complicated, beautiful, and human life really is. You get to see different places, different struggles, different ways of living — but somehow it always comes back to the same thing: people trying their best, loving their families, surviving, laughing, dreaming, and finding meaning.

It genuinely makes me feel more connected to the world. Thank you for giving us these stories and for reminding us how beautiful life can be when we take the time to listen to others.
author: @SvenKarlsson80

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: My go to channel to see how others are living around the country this channel has shown me ways of living I didn’t think existed anymore
author: @markblack4017

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: My family moved to Arkansas from NYC over 20 years ago, I was still in high school and absolutely hated it. After all this time now, Little Rock has become my heart and home. No matter where I go in life, I’ll always find my way back! ♥️
author: @thebunnyjulie

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: I am not American but this is the most beautiful part of USA I’ve ever seen… the architectural design is amazing
author: @DagenLucas

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: I grew up near Little Rock in Hot Springs and I got so excited to finally see videos on Arkansas.  We seriously go unnoticed.  Thanks, Peter!  I love my home state and hope to return soon.  Shout out to all the Germans that watch this channel.  Das ist super toll!
author: @Lollardy1234

title: Inside Arkansas - First Impressions (Little Rock)
body: I’ve watched you for years, Peter.  My husband and I moved to Ark. during the pandemic.  We’re approx 2 hours from LR.  LOVE Arkansas, a hidden gem.  Kudos to you for highlighting these beautiful folks with such dignity and respect.  This woman at minute 26 is incredible.  Lovely people here.  ❤
author: @kimberlypotts8922

title: Ultimate Boston Campus Tour #boston #mit #bostoncollege #bu
body: Why should we take you opinion on any of your reviews. What are your qualifications? Who asked you to do this? What’s your major? title: @TheNepaliAmerican Her undergrad journey in Arkansas State University//Honest Guidance to Nepali
body: As an US applicant, most of the days i feel so hopeless and demotivated and so tensed regarding refusal or acceptance of visa . whenever your video pops up , i could never ignore it even in my bad mood, you boost my energy tashii. I guess i have always watched your videos since preparing for ielts to starting the process of us, so you have always been my motivation through my journey . i just want to thank you a lot youre a great motivation to many people, just always make yourself proud and happy to be in that place where we students dream being there. watching you progressing also makes us happy and motivated. so happy seeing you putting efforts for us, god bless you tashii.
